content: The core limitation of 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory—failure to retain and prioritize low-reward, uncertain exploratory experiences that drive long-term task generalization—can be resolved by tiering curiosity-driven intrinsic reward signals from ICM and RND methods directly into memory retrieval gating. Agents would only move high-curiosity (uncertain, novel) unlabeled experiences from short-term episodic buffer to long-term persistent memory, prioritizing exploration targets that align with active learning’s core goal of maximizing knowledge gain per data sample.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
